raw_consumer_statement:
On our about XX/XX/XXXX I noticed my Best Buy credit card was being used by someone in XXXX. While paying my bill I noticed a large online purchase that I was not aware of for {$3000.00}. I contacted Best Buy directly on or about XXXX of XXXX. During that time several more purchases were made online not by me and returned in XXXX XXXX Best Buy opened a fraud case and issued a new card. The day after I received the new card I again noticed several online purchases made by someone other than me. Best Buy again issued a new card and credited my account. 

The following is a summary of fraudulent purchases made : XX/XX/XXXX - Online purchase of XXXX -Returned item in XXXX XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX for XXXX XX/XX/XXXX - Online purchase for XXXX XX/XX/XXXX - Online purchase for XXXX XX/XX/XXXX - A return in XXXX XXXX for XXXX XX/XX/XXXX - Online purchase for XXXX XX/XX/XXXX - Online purchase for XXXX XX/XX/XXXX - Online purchase for XXXX XX/XX/XXXX - Online purchase for XXXX XX/XX/XXXX - Online purchase for XXXX After filing a dispute I was credited back {$130.00}. The letter stated I did not have sufficient evidence. I have not made a purchase from Best Buy since XX/XX/XXXX. I have received no items from online purchases, and I know no one in XXXXXXXX XXXX 


On Wednesday I received a fraud alert from Best Buy asking if I was aware of a {$2100.00} purchase in XXXX I told them no and they again issued a new card. They were able to stop the transaction from going through. My goal is to clear the fraudulent debt, pay off the amount I owe and then cancel the card since I've had so many issues in the past few months with little help. I have file a police report with my local police department.
